title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline; isolate the first causal fault in buildkit graphs, cache, multi-stage builds, mounts, and secret boundaries and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.
Predict cache keys for instructions, files, metadata, arguments, mounts, and parent state and isolate frequently changing inputs.
Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Docker External Cache Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
Order the Docker External Cache Reproducibility container delivery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Docker External Cache Reproducibility.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Docker External Cache Reproducibility assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Docker External Cache Reproducibility, keep Docker External Cache Reproducibility connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Docker External Cache Reproducibility has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker External Cache Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-external-cache-reproducibility-1ekzb3". Tailor its Dockerfile.buildkit change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker External Cache Reproducibility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker External Cache Reproducibility into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker External Cache Reproducibility, keep Docker External Cache Reproducibility connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-external-cache-reproducibility-1ekzb3
  - competency: docker-external-cache-reproducibility
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: external cache reproducibility
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: remote context crossing a separately authorized daemon boundary
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: 1ekzb3
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
